rationale: Operator can follow both paths cleanly: Runner dispatch reads `runner:` via `mindspec config show`, the three enum values match internal/config/config.go's validated set exactly, claude-code-skills is confirmed the code-level default, and the four superseded-callouts (Launch/Codex-failure/Workdir/Anti-patterns) correctly point workflow-path readers at /ms-panel instead. Mirror byte-identity holds, build is clean. But the workflow-path invocation text in ms-panel-run/SKILL.md only names `{slug, spec, target, bead_id?, round, lenses[], mix}` — it never mentions resolving `claude_sub_on_quota`, even though workflow/interfaces.md (this same bead's doc-sync, lines 236-238) explicitly assigns that resolution job to ms-panel-run: 'mix and claude_sub_on_quota are resolved by the invoking skill (ms-panel-run, spec 111 Bead 3) from config panel:/panel.substitution.claude_sub_on_quota'. An operator following only the SKILL.md prose (not the interfaces.md doc) will omit the arg; the workflow's fail-closed default then silently turns off quota-wall substitution even when the operator has `claude_sub_on_quota: true` set, contradicting the same sentence's claim that the workflow runs 'the quota-wall-substitution ladder ... itself.' Safe failure mode (missing slot -> gate blocks), not a correctness break, but a real, fixable operator-usability gap the BRIEF specifically asked me to check for.
concrete_changes_required: In both ms-panel-run/SKILL.md mirrors' Runner dispatch bullet for claude-code-workflow, add `claude_sub_on_quota` to the resolved-args tuple and one clause instructing the operator to resolve it from `panel.substitution.claude_sub_on_quota` (mirroring how `mix` is already resolved from `panel:`), so the workflow's advertised quota-wall-substitution ladder actually engages when configured.
findings: ms-panel-run/SKILL.md Runner dispatch text omits `claude_sub_on_quota` from the /ms-panel invocation args, though workflow/interfaces.md assigns this exact bead the job of resolving+passing it; the workflow's fail-closed default (absent -> false) means the promised substitution ladder silently never fires on the workflow path unless an operator independently reads interfaces.md and adds the arg themselves.

Positive: the three runner branches (claude-code-workflow/claude-code-skills/external) exactly match internal/config/config.go's validated enum; claude-code-skills is confirmed the code default (config.go:336,477); the host-degrades-to-skills-path fallback line is present and reasonable.

Positive: the workflow/skills-path handoff to /ms-panel lines up on every arg it does mention (slug, spec, target, bead_id?, round, lenses[], mix), invoked once, per ms-panel.js's documented args contract and buildCommand chokepoint; word-boundary grep confirms no /ms-panel-run or /ms-panel-tally substring false-matches the /ms-panel workflow reference.

Positive: orchestrators (ms-bead-cycle, ms-spec-final-review) reference /ms-panel-run and /ms-panel-tally by name only and never inspect their internals, so the new internal runner-branching is fully transparent to composition -- no orchestrator update needed or missing.
Positive: mirror byte-identity holds for both SKILL.md pairs and for .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js vs plugins/mindspec/workflows/ms-panel.js; go build ./... is clean.

The tally's Workflow-path note correctly describes consuming pre-rendered verify+tally output without skipping the artifact-gate screen or Consolidate step -- coherent and accurately scoped to Step 1 only.

The external-runner stub is coherent: documented out-of-scope, degrades to human/skills-path per ADR-0040 (file exists at .mindspec/adr/ADR-0040-orchestration-layering-ratchet.md), no dangling reference.

Step-0-labelling judgment call (BRIEF item 6): acceptable as-is -- the Runner-dispatch section (read first per its own instruction) already tells the workflow-path reader not to walk 'Step 0 through the Anti-patterns section', which covers Step 0 by range even without an inline callout on the Step 0 heading itself. A reader who jumps straight to Step 0 without reading Runner dispatch first would miss this, but Runner dispatch is positioned first and says to read it 'before doing anything else', so this is a minor readability nit, not a defect.
